title: rootless: create user conf files when they don't exist
body:
If the configuration files for the user don't exist, create them automatically.

Different policies are used for the creation of these files:

- `storage.conf`: use the default configuration known to libpod at the time these files are created.  This will prevent that users will keep using their configuration if we decide to change any default value in future.
- `libpod.conf`: same policy as `storage.conf`.
- `registries.conf`: when the file is not found, the global configuration is used.
- `policy.json`: same policy as `registries.conf`.
- `mounts.conf`: it is created as an empty file.

comments.body:
My concern about this is that the users will not get advantage of any changes to these files, if they never modified them, themselves.

Can we generate a symbolic link and then when the user modifies them we will know.  We could document that.  I think only storage.conf is necessary to be different?
the only file that could take advantage of that is `registries.conf`, all other ones must be customized for each user.  Should we treat `registries.conf` differently than other files?

Ok, I am wondering if we need a podman reset command or something like that.  We currently have issues with random content in the users homedir, that is somewhat difficult for the user to know how to cleanup.  
If we go with this change, we should probably add info rot podman info to show that its registries.conf is different then the hosts registries.conf.   Or just drop registries.conf from this patch altogether and document if the  user wants to use something different. I don't see this happening often.
ok I've dropped the patch for copying `registries.conf`.  If the file is not found now in the user configuration, the global configuration is used
Do you need to worry about /etc/containers/policy.json?
Changes LGTM, but would like other head nods on this.

> Do you need to worry about /etc/containers/policy.json?

I forgot about it, thanks to have pointed out.

I think the behaviour at this time is correct, similarly to `registries.conf`, when it is not customized the global file is used.

> Changes LGTM, but would like other head nods on this.

and I'd definitely like to know what @mheon thinks about this PR before it is merged
LGTM - I think this will help a lot with ensuring rootless files always go in the same place.
can we cover this at a scrum before merging?
/approve cancel

> can we cover this at a scrum before merging?

sure.  Is there anything in particular that must be covered?
mostly that @mheon is working on a PR that will be complimentary to this and we should make sure things are coordinated on how they work. no major objections at all.

Sorry, I am late to the game. Two things came to my mind while reading the code:

- Currently, we are ignoring errors when creating those files. I don't understand why they're being ignored but I likely miss some background story. Can you elaborate on that?

- Another issue relates to update scenarios. Not having those config files might be intended by some users who want to rely on Podman's defaults. When creating the config files, Podman will continue loading the defaults from this specific version of Podman.

The issue with relying on the defaults is when they change - almost all of
them changing will result in breaking changes that lose all the user's
rootless containers.

Thanks, @mheon. Makes sense. Better safe than sorry :+1:

Right I believe we should only change the config files that "rootless" podman overrides from the user installed version.

This is why I don't want @giuseppe creating a registries.conf or policy.json, we should continue to use the system defaults unless the user creates them.

BTW I don't think we should ignore the errors when creating these files.
/approve
